title: arceuthobium globosum subsp. aureum
intro: Arceuthobium globosum subsp. aureum, also known as the golden dwarf mistletoe, is a type of parasitic plant that belongs to the family Santalaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. The herbal preparations of Arceuthobium globosum subsp. aureum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
